name: Red Wine and Mushroom Risotto
recipeIngredient: 2 cups dry red wine (e.g., Rosso di Montalcino)
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: Use a quality red wine such as Rosso di Montalcino.
1. In a small bowl, soak the dried porcini in boiling water for 30 minutes. Drain through a fine sieve lined with a dampened paper towel or coffee filter into a bowl and reserve. Rinse the porcini under cold water to remove grit, then pat dry and chop finely.
2. Chop the remaining cremini or white mushrooms (about 1-1/2 cups) and set aside.
3. In a large heavy skillet, cook the pancetta over moderate heat with salt and pepper until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Add chopped mushrooms, reserving them in the skillet for later use.
4. On a large stovetop pan, bring broth to a bare simmer.
5. In a 5- to 6-quart heavy saucepan, cook pancetta over moderate heat, stirring until crispy, about 5 minutes. Add reserved chopped mushrooms, remaining butter, onion, rosemary, sage, and salt and pepper. Cook until the onion is softened.
6. Stir in rice and cook until well coated with fat, about 1 minute. Add 1 cup of wine and cook, constantly stirring, until absorbed.
7. Add the remaining cup of wine and cook, constantly stirring, until absorbed.
8. Gradually add 1 cup of simmering broth, allowing each addition to be fully absorbed before adding the next, until rice is tender but still al dente, about 20-25 minutes.
9. About halfway through cooking, stir in the reserved porcini soaking liquid and chopped porcini, continuing to cook until the liquid is absorbed.
10. During the last few minutes of cooking, reheat the reserved mushrooms in a skillet over moderate heat, stirring until hot. Stir in parsley, salt, pepper, and grated Parmesan. Serve risotto immediately, topped with reheated mushrooms and garnished with Parmesan curls and fresh rosemary sprigs. Serves 6.
